Identity.History: Cambyses II was king of the Achaemenid Persian Empire from 530–522 BC and the son of Cyrus the Great. He is best known for conquering Egypt in 525 BC, defeating Pharaoh Psamtik III and adding the ancient kingdom to the Persian Empire. Greek sources portray him as cruel and unstable, though these accounts may be exaggerated or hostile. While returning from Egypt, Cambyses died under unclear circumstances as a revolt broke out in Persia. His death led to a succession crisis and the rise of Darius I.
